rationale: The cited paper is used repeatedly as a core prior benchmark for the long-term promotion literature, including the claim that permanent effects are usually rare and the distinction between cumulative versus permanent effects. The citing paper also follows the same persistence-modeling/impulse-response tradition and explicitly adopts interpretation conventions attributed to Pauwels, Hanssens, and Siddarth (2002). It then positions its contribution as moving beyond that prior stream by analyzing many more (including smaller) brands and linking long-term promotion effects to brand equity and innovation.
relation_description: The citing paper builds directly on Pauwels, Hanssens, and Siddarth (2002) as a methodological and empirical baseline for long-term promotion effects, then adds new brand-level moderators and a broader brand sample. In this sense, it extends that line of work rather than merely listing it as background.
